title: Nut Butter Cups
summary: Salty, snappy and loaded with nut butter. A twist on the classic choc treat, made ahead and ready whenever you need a sweet hit.

method.description: Blend everything except the chocolate and coconut oil until you get a smooth, sticky texture.

Add a spoonful of the mixture into each hole of a silicone mini cupcake tray. Press down until compact. Dollop a teaspoon of nut butter into the centre of each, then repeat with the remaining mixture until you have filled up each cup. Bang the tray on your work surface to help the nut butter settle into the base. Pop into the freezer for 30 mins.
Meanwhile, melt the dark chocolate and coconut oil in the microwave in 30-second bursts, or over a bain-marie, until smooth. Pour the chocolate into a jug.
Take the tray out of the freezer and pour a little melted chocolate over each cup to cover the nut butter. Tap the tray gently to even out the tops. Return to the freezer until set solid.
Once frozen, pop the nut butter cups out of the tray. Store them in the freezer, or keep in the fridge if you're eating them soon. These last for a week in the fridge and 3 months in the freezer.

tips: You’ll need a silicone mini cupcake tray.
Store in the freezer for up to 1 month, perfect for snacking on the go.
Let them sit at room temp for 5-10 mins before eating if frozen solid.

chefs.name: Rachel Pamart
chefs.summary: Rachel is the founder of Rawlala, a cold-pressed juicery based in East London. She came to clean, holistic eating by tuning into what made her feel most alive and now she channels that into expert, ingredient-led recipes designed to nourish, energise, and taste damn good.
